rulings.Case Title: CONS. OF JOHN ALEXANDER JAMESON
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/14/2021. 1) The ending balance of assets in interim and final accounts filed by fiduciaries, who are not financial institutions, must be verified. [LRSF 14.35 D.] 2) In the Trustee timesheets please explain each entry for 6 hours for "Visit JJ" and explain why a 6-hour visits is appropriate. 6-hour visits appear excessive. The court is unable to determine 6 hours is appropriate based on the timesheets only saying "Visit JJ" for 6 hours. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 6/1/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.]

rulings.Rulings: Appearance required. Issues to include: 1) Petitioner requests $121,525 in fees (1% guideline), less $35,200 already paid, for total remaining of $86,325.19. 2) Petitioner has paid himself $35,200 and Petitioner requests authority to continue to pay himself on account per 7/14/10 order; however, the order settling the prior (5th) accounting allowed only $1,200/month ($28,800 for this 24-month accounting period). Remote appearances should be made whenever possible. Please review the 9th amended general order. The following information pertains to appearances by Zoom: WHEN ENTERING "YOUR NAME" ON ZOOM, BOTH THE FIRST AND LAST NAME, AS WELL THE CASE NUMBER, MUST BE INCLUDED. Meeting ID: 875 8017 7925 Password: 722809 To appear by telephone through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833, and follow the prompts, using the meeting ID and password above. Information regarding appearance by CourtCall can be found in the 9th amended general order.

(Except for exhibits, each paper filed with the court must bear a footer in the bottom margin of each page, placed below the page number and divided from the rest of the document page by a printed line. rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF YOUNG GOON LI
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Report, Fees, Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/9/2021. Endorsed-filed single sided courtesy copies are required for any papers filed with the court and must be received by Examiner in room 202 at the Superior Court. Examiners will not review any document until after it has been filed and a courtesy copy has been received. [LRSF 2.7, 14.7]. Courtesy copies dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays. Petitioner must submit a hard copy of the proposed order to the Examiner in room 202 at least five (5) court days in advance of the scheduled hearing date, with the scheduled hearing date noted on the face sheet. [LRSF 14.7]. Proposed orders dropped off without a hearing date or examiner name on the top cover page or on the envelope, will be processed by the clerks as time allows and may cause delays.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF ESTHER SOO HOO
rulings.Calendar Matter: ACCOUNTING PETITION RE: Final Distribution
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/28/21 for an amended petition to be filed and noticed. The petition is not properly verified. No proposed order has been submitted. [LRSF 14.7 A.] If the personal representative is a creditor, the clerk shall present the claim to the court or judge for approval or rejection. The court or judge may in its discretion require the creditor to file a petition and give notice of hearing. [Probate Code Section 9252.] Prob. C. Sect. 9202(b) also requires notice if an heir or beneficiary has previously been confined in a prison or facility under the jurisdiction of the Department of Corrections, etc. There was no Exhibit A attached to the petition. Notice was mailed to James Soo Hoo at a different address than the one shown in the petition. A petition for final distribution must state specifically how the estate is to be distributed by showing the distributee's name and description of the property, including the complete description of each individual security and the net amount of cash (as of a date certain) distributable to each beneficiary. [CRC 7.651; LRSF 14.36 I.6.] The initial Petition for Probate listed Jeannie Soo Hoo as the decedent's mother, not sister. Paragraph 31 of the petition is incorrect, as Jeannie Soo Hoo was not included among the beneficiaries of the residue. If the general pecuniary bequests have not yet been made, they must be made with interest now as more than a year has passed since the date of death. [Prob. C. Sect. 12003.] (Item 4 of an I&A should NOT be completed when a Probate Referee HAS BEEN appointed. And Rev. & Tax. Code Sect. 19513 was repealed in 2018.)

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF HENRY Z. HELMER SR.
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Hearing. Appearance required. PARTIES APPEARING SHOULD CHECK IN FOR THE HEARING WITH THEIR FULL NAME. Counsel and parties must, whenever possible, appear remotely through Zoom or CourtCall. For a video appearance, go to zoom.us, click "join a meeting" and input meeting ID 875 8017 7925 and password 722809. For an audio-only appearance through Zoom, call 1-669-900-6833 and key in meeting ID "875 8017 7925#", then participant ID "0#", then password "722809#". (Toll rates may apply).) The Court prefers that counsel and parties appear by video and that they input their first and last name into the "Your Name" dialogue box. Counsel and parties may appear by audio-only telephone through CourtCall by calling 1-888-882-6878. Please review the Court's 9th Amended General Order which took effect on 02/10/2021 for more information.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE MARINA KUAN LIVING TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Removal Of Trustee
rulings.Rulings: continued to 6/23/21. no proof of service of the petition on file (30 days notice is required). no file stamped copy of the petition and no proposed order delivered to the examiner. all issues must be cured no later than 6/14/21 in order to avoid another automatic continuance.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE THE DELORISA TURNER TRUST UNDER AGREEMENT DATED TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition To Determine Ownership Of Trust Property, For Specific Performance Of Executory Contract Of Sale Of Trust Property And For Order Authorizing
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/21/2021. 1) Notice of the hearing with a copy of the petition must be served on Dames Shouman and currently acting trustee of the decedent's trust in accordance with Probate Code section 851(a). In addition, notice of the hearing with a copy of the petition must be served on each person listed in Probate Code section 17203 if the matter concerns a trust. Probate Code section 851(b)(3). 2) If the lease was not assigned to the decedent's trust, the personal representative of the decedent's estate must be served in accordance with Probate Code section 851(a). If there is no probate, each beneficiary of the estate must be served in the same manner. 3) Item 1 of the DE-115 form must state the complete title of the pleading to which the notice relates. CRC 7.50. 4) The title of the pleading identified in the petitioner's verification does not appear identical to the title of the petition. 5) No proposed order has been provided by any party. 6) No courtesy copies of documents filed by Dames Shouman have been received. 7) Dames Shouman's opposition must be verified. 8) No courtesy copies of documents filed by the decedent's trust have been received. 9) The decedent's trust's opposition must be verified by the currently acting trustee. The trustee, rather than the trust, must be identified as the objector/respondent in pleadings.

rulings.Case Title: IN THE MATTER OF THE OF CONNIE C. CHUN TRUST
rulings.Calendar Matter: Petition For Order Confirming Trustee And Trust Assets
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 06/14/2021. 1) No courtesy copy of the petition has been received. 2) No proposed order has been received. 3) Notice must be given on a mandatory Judicial Council form DE-115. 4) A verified supplement addressing the following issues must be filed: A) Please provide the name and date of death of grantor's predeceased spouse. B) Please provide a copy of the grantor's will. If probated, please provide a copy of letters issued to the personal representative. C) Please identify the names, ages, relationships, and addresses of all individuals who would be heirs if the grantor had died intestate.

rulings.Case Title: THE ESTATE OF JESSIE MAE RUSH
rulings.Calendar Matter: PETITION FOR LETTERS OF ADMINISTRATION WITH WILL ANNEXED
rulings.Rulings: Continued to 6/9/2021. 1) The petition must be amended to request appointment of a person as personal representative not a law firm. An amended pleading or an amendment to a pleading requires the same notice of hearing (including publication) as the pleading it amends. [CRC 7.53]. 2) Notice does not indicate Lost Will. Petitions for probate of lost wills must clearly state on their face that the will is lost and both the published and mailed notice must so state. [LRSF 14.20]. 3) Must file Duties and Liabilities of Personal Representative (DE-147). 4) Confidential Statement of Birth Date and Driver's License Number (Judicial Council Form DE-147S) is also required, pursuant to Probate Code 8404(b). Form DE-147S must be filed separately and confidentially. Do not file form DE-147S as an attachment to the Duties and Liabilities form. [LRSF 14.18 E.] 5) Please amend attachment 3f(3) as it must address the factors and presumption in Probate Code 6124. 6) Please provide legal authority for petitioner having priority as personal representative. An endorsed-filed single sided hard copy of all documents filed in response to these notes, as well as any other documents required must be received by this Examiner in room 202 no later than 4 pm on 5/24/2021 [LRSF 14.7 B.]
